info.title: Orpheus API
info.description:
The Orpheus API provides asynchronous audio processing as a multi-tenant SaaS. Upload raw audio, submit processing jobs (transcription, enhancement, separation, classification, and more), and consume results via polling or webhooks.

## Authentication

All `/v1/...` endpoints require authentication. Two schemes are supported:

- **Keycloak JWT** — a bearer token obtained from your org's Keycloak realm. Used for browser-based and user-context access.
- **API key** — a long-lived token (prefix `ak_live_...` or `ak_test_...`) issued via `POST /v1/api-keys`. Used for server-to-server and CI/CD integrations. The full secret is returned exactly once at creation; Orpheus stores only a salted hash.

A request may present either scheme via the appropriate header. Endpoints that mint credentials (`POST /v1/api-keys`) require a Keycloak JWT so that an interactive user is always in the loop.

## Rate limits

Every org has per-minute and per-hour rate limits that apply across all `/v1/...` endpoints. Limits depend on the org's plan and are reported in the `X-RateLimit-Limit`, `X-RateLimit-Remaining`, and `X-RateLimit-Reset` response headers. Exceeding a limit returns `429 Too Many Requests` with a `Retry-After` header.

## Idempotency

The endpoints that create resources (`POST /v1/uploads`, `POST /v1/jobs`, `POST /v1/jobs/bulk`, `POST /v1/webhooks`, `POST /v1/api-keys`) accept an optional `Idempotency-Key` request header (1–255 characters; a UUIDv4 is recommended). When the same key is replayed with the same body within the 24-hour retention window, the original response is returned without re-executing the operation. Replaying the same key with a different body returns `409 Conflict`.

## Webhooks

Orpheus delivers asynchronous events (job lifecycle, upload completion, billing) to HTTPS endpoints registered via `POST /v1/webhooks`. Each delivery includes an `Orpheus-Signature` header (HMAC-SHA256 of the body, keyed on the endpoint's secret) and a monotonically increasing `Orpheus-Delivery-Id`. Deliveries are retried with exponential backoff for up to 24 hours; inspect `GET /v1/webhooks/{id}/deliveries` for the full history and trigger redelivery via `POST /v1/webhooks/{id}/deliveries/{delivery_id}/replay`.

## Versioning

The API is versioned via the URL prefix. The current major version is `v1`. Breaking changes will ship under a new prefix; additive changes roll out under `v1` with a deprecation notice in the `Sunset` header and a `Deprecation` notice in the response body.

tags.name: uploads
tags.description: Multipart uploads to S3 via presigned URLs.

tags.name: artifacts
tags.description: Audio artifacts stored in object storage and their derived metadata.

tags.name: api-keys
tags.description: Programmatic API keys for non-interactive access.

tags.name: processors
tags.description: Catalog of available audio processing operations and their versions.

tags.name: system
tags.description: Org-scoped operational endpoints: usage, audit log, and platform health.

get.description: Returns 200 OK if the process is alive. Container orchestrators (Kubernetes, ECS) use this endpoint to decide whether to restart the container. It must not depend on external services and requires no authentication.
Returns 200 OK if the service is ready to accept traffic. In Phase 0 this always returns ready; Phase 1+ checks Postgres, Redis, and S3. Requires no authentication.

503.description: Service is not ready. Returned when a downstream dependency is unhealthy.

get.description: Prometheus exposition format. Includes Go runtime and process metrics by default. Phase 1+ registers domain-specific counters and histograms (jobs submitted, jobs succeeded, GPU seconds consumed, etc.). Requires no authentication.

200.description: Metrics in Prometheus text exposition format.

schema.example:
# HELP go_goroutines Number of goroutines that currently exist.
# TYPE go_goroutines gauge
go_goroutines 7

post.summary: Create an upload session and receive presigned S3 multipart URLs
post.description:
Initiates a multipart upload to S3. The response includes a presigned URL for each part, which the client uses to `PUT` the corresponding byte range directly to S3. Call `POST /v1/uploads/{id}/complete` once all parts have been uploaded.

Supports the `Idempotency-Key` header. Replaying the same key with the same body within 24 hours returns the original response. Replaying with a different body returns `409 Conflict`.

201.description: Upload session created. The `Location` header points to the session resource; `Idempotency-Key` is echoed when present.

get.summary: List upload sessions for the authenticated org
get.description: Returns upload sessions owned by the org, newest first. Supports cursor-based pagination.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter by upload session status.

parameters.name: created_after
parameters.description: Only return sessions created at or after this timestamp (RFC 3339).

parameters.name: created_before
parameters.description: Only return sessions created at or before this timestamp (RFC 3339).

get.description: Returns the current state of a multipart upload session, including the presigned part URLs (which are regenerated if they have expired).

post.summary: Finalize the upload, verify checksums, and create the artifact
post.description: Marks the multipart upload as complete in S3 and verifies the SHA-256 of the assembled object (if provided at creation time). On success, an `Artifact` record is created and returned. The artifact's storage is charged to the org from this moment forward.

get.description: Returns artifacts owned by the org, newest first. Supports cursor-based pagination and filtering by content type and creation timestamp.

parameters.name: content_type
parameters.description: Filter by MIME type (e.g. `audio/wav`, `audio/mpeg`).

parameters.name: created_after
parameters.description: Only return artifacts created at or after this timestamp (RFC 3339).

parameters.name: created_before
parameters.description: Only return artifacts created at or before this timestamp (RFC 3339).

get.description: Returns metadata for a single artifact, including codec, duration, sample rate, and channels extracted by the ingestion probe.
Returns a short-lived S3 presigned `GET` URL. Default TTL is 15 minutes; the maximum is 1 hour.

parameters.name: expires_in
parameters.description: URL lifetime in seconds. Must be between 60 and 3600.

post.description:
Submits a job that runs a named processor against an existing artifact. Returns immediately with status `queued`; poll the job URL or subscribe to webhooks to learn when it completes.

Supports the `Idempotency-Key` header. Replaying the same key with the same body within 24 hours returns the original response. Replaying with a different body returns `409 Conflict`.

202.description: Job accepted and queued. The `Location` header points to the job resource; `Idempotency-Key` is echoed when present.

get.description: Returns jobs owned by the org, newest first. Supports cursor-based pagination and filtering by status, processor, artifact, and creation timestamp.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter by job status.

parameters.name: processor
parameters.description: Filter by processor name (e.g. `whisper-transcribe`).

parameters.name: artifact_id
parameters.description: Filter by source artifact ID.

parameters.name: created_after
parameters.description: Only return jobs created at or after this timestamp (RFC 3339).

parameters.name: created_before
parameters.description: Only return jobs created at or before this timestamp (RFC 3339).

post.description:
Accepts up to 500 jobs in a single request. Per-item validation failures are returned in the `rejected` array; the rest are queued. Use the `Idempotency-Key` header to make the entire batch replayable.

Supports the `Idempotency-Key` header. Replaying the same key with the same body within 24 hours returns the original response. Replaying with a different body returns `409 Conflict`.

202.description: Batch accepted. `accepted` is the list of queued job IDs in the same order as the input; `rejected` contains per-item validation errors.

get.description: Returns the full job record, including `result` (populated when `status = succeeded`), `error` (populated when `status = failed`), and `cost_usd`.

post.summary: Request cancellation of a queued or running job
post.description: Returns `202` with the job in `canceled` (if it had not started) or `canceling` (if a worker has already picked it up; the worker will be asked to stop at the next checkpoint).

Registers an HTTPS endpoint to receive Orpheus event deliveries. If `secret` is omitted, Orpheus generates a 32-byte secret and returns it in the response. The same secret is used to sign every delivery (HMAC-SHA256).

Supports the `Idempotency-Key` header. Replaying the same key with the same body within 24 hours returns the original response. Replaying with a different body returns `409 Conflict`.

get.description: Returns every webhook endpoint registered for the org.
Returns a single webhook endpoint, including its current `active` flag and the timestamp of the most recent successful delivery.

patch.description: Patches one or more of `subscribed_events`, `active`, `description`, or `url`. Only the fields present in the request body are modified.

delete.description: Permanently deletes a webhook endpoint. In-flight deliveries will be allowed to complete; no new deliveries will be enqueued.

get.description: Returns the most recent deliveries (and their attempts) for debugging and replay. Supports cursor-based pagination.

parameters.name: event_type
parameters.description: Filter by event type (e.g. `job.succeeded`).

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter by delivery status.

post.description: Enqueues a fresh delivery for a previously sent event. The new delivery is created with `attempt_count = 0` and `status = pending`; the original delivery record is preserved for audit.

Mints a new API key for the org. The full `secret` is returned **exactly once** in the response and is never stored in plaintext; subsequent reads return only the prefix and metadata. This endpoint requires a Keycloak JWT (an interactive user must mint the key); API keys cannot mint other API keys.

Supports the `Idempotency-Key` header. Replaying the same key with the same body within 24 hours returns the original response. Replaying with a different body returns `409 Conflict`.

201.description: API key created. The `secret` field contains the full token; capture it now — it will not be shown again.

get.description: Returns metadata for every API key in the org. Secrets are never returned by this endpoint; revoke and reissue if a secret is lost.

delete.description: Immediately invalidates the key. In-flight requests using the key complete normally; subsequent requests return `401`.

get.description: Returns a summary of every processor the org is allowed to use, including the latest version and the set of currently active versions.

get.summary: Describe a processor and its available versions
get.description: Returns the full record for a processor, including every published version, its parameter schema, and its per-second cost.

get.summary: Get current billing-period usage for the authenticated org
get.description: Returns aggregate usage and dollar cost for the current billing period, with a per-category breakdown.

parameters.name: period
parameters.description: Which billing period to summarize. `current` (default) or an ISO-8601 month (`YYYY-MM`).

get.summary: List audit log entries for the authenticated org
get.description: Returns the org's audit log, newest first. Each entry records the actor (user or API key), the action, the affected resource, and the source IP / user agent. Supports cursor-based pagination.

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: Filter by action (e.g. `api_key.create`, `job.cancel`).

parameters.name: actor_id
parameters.description: Filter by the actor's user or API key ID.

parameters.name: resource_type
parameters.description: Filter by resource type (e.g. `job`, `artifact`, `webhook`).

parameters.name: created_after
parameters.description: Only return entries created at or after this timestamp (RFC 3339).

parameters.name: created_before
parameters.description: Only return entries created at or before this timestamp (RFC 3339).

200.description: A page of audit log entries.

get.summary: List invoices for the authenticated org
get.description: Returns the org's invoices, newest billing period first. Each invoice is a per-period rollup of completed-job cost.

post.summary: Open a hosted checkout for an invoice
post.description: Creates a payment-provider hosted checkout for an open invoice and returns its URL. Returns the existing URL if a checkout was already opened. 503 if billing is not configured.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Invoice id.

post.description: Receives payment events from Dodo Payments. Authenticated by the Standard Webhooks signature in the request headers (not an API key). Settles the matching invoice on a successful payment.

get.description: Cache hit/miss counts, hit rate, and estimated savings for the current billing period (PRD 01).

delete.summary: Invalidate cache entries for a model version
delete.description: Purge content-cache entries produced by a given model_version_id (admin). Org-scoped.

post.description: Zip a set of artifacts (and optionally source-job result JSON) into one signed, expiring download. Async: returns 202 + poll_url.

get.summary: Download a bundle (302 to signed URL)

302.description: Redirect to a signed S3 URL.

200.description: Enriched delivery detail (attempt timeline, signature base string, response snippet).

BearerAuth.description: Keycloak-issued JWT. Obtain via your org's Keycloak realm's standard OAuth2/OIDC flow and pass in the `Authorization` header as `Bearer <token>`.

ApiKeyAuth.name: X-API-Key
ApiKeyAuth.description: Prefix-scoped API key (e.g. `ak_live_xxx`). Issued via `POST /v1/api-keys`; the full secret is returned exactly once at creation.

IdempotencyKey.name: Idempotency-Key
IdempotencyKey.description: Optional client-supplied key that makes the request safely retryable. 1–255 characters; a UUIDv4 is recommended. Replaying the same key with the same body within 24 hours returns the original response. Replaying with a different body returns `409 Conflict`.

Limit.name: limit
Limit.description: Maximum number of items to return. Default 50, maximum 200.

Cursor.name: cursor
Cursor.description: Opaque pagination cursor returned as `next_cursor` in the previous page. Omit to fetch the first page.

BadRequestProblem.description: Request validation failed. See `errors` for field-level details.

NotFoundProblem.description: The requested resource does not exist or is not visible to the authenticated org.

ConflictProblem.description: The request conflicts with the current state of the target resource (e.g. idempotency-key reuse with a different body, or cancellation of a finished job).

PayloadTooLargeProblem.description: The requested upload exceeds the org's maximum artifact size.

RateLimitedProblem.description: Rate limit exceeded. See `Retry-After` for when to retry.

Location.description: Canonical URL of the created resource.

IdempotencyKey.description: Echoes the `Idempotency-Key` request header so clients can confirm which key the server associated with the response.

RetryAfter.description: Seconds to wait before retrying. Present on 429 and 503 responses.

type.description: URI identifying the problem type. Should resolve to human-readable documentation.

title.description: Short, human-readable summary of the problem type.

status.description: HTTP status code generated by the origin server.

detail.description: Human-readable explanation specific to this occurrence.

instance.description: URI identifying this specific occurrence of the problem.

errors.description: Optional field-level error details, primarily populated for 400 responses.

ErrorField.description: A single field-level validation error, embedded in a `Problem`.

field.description: Dotted path to the offending field.

status.description: Static liveness token. Always "ok" when the process is running.
Aggregate readiness verdict. "ready" iff every check is "ok".

checks.description: Per-dependency readiness results, keyed by dependency name.

filename.description: Original filename. Stored as artifact metadata; not used for storage path.

content_type.description: IANA MIME type. Must be a supported audio type.

size_bytes.description: Total object size in bytes. Must not exceed the org's quota.

sha256.description: Optional client-computed SHA-256 of the assembled object. Verified during completion; a mismatch causes the upload to fail.

part_size.description: Byte size that the client must use for each part.

parts.description: One entry per part. `part_number` is 1-indexed; `url` is a presigned S3 `PUT` URL valid until `expires_at`.

expires_at.description: When the session itself expires. After this time the session can no longer be completed.

fields.description: Optional extra form fields the client must include with each part `PUT` (currently always empty for S3 multipart).

Part.description: A single part in a multipart upload session.

part_number.description: 1-indexed part number, matching the byte range sent to `url`.

url.description: Presigned S3 `PUT` URL for this part.

expires_at.description: When this individual part URL expires.

parts.description: List of parts in ascending `part_number` order. The S3 `ETag` returned by each part `PUT` must be supplied verbatim.

CompletedPart.description: An uploaded part being reported back to the server.

etag.description: S3 `ETag` header value for this part, as returned by S3.

UploadStatus.description: Lifecycle state of an upload session.

has_more.description: Whether another page is available after this one.

next_cursor.description: Opaque cursor to pass back as `cursor` to fetch the next page. `null` when `has_more` is `false`.

Artifact.description: A stored audio artifact and its derived metadata.

content_type.description: MIME type as supplied at upload time.

codec.description: Audio codec extracted by the ingestion probe.

filename.description: Original filename supplied at upload time.

SignedURL.description: A presigned S3 `GET` URL for an artifact.

ProcessorRef.description: A reference to a named processor at a specific version.

artifact_id.description: ID of the artifact to process.

params.description: Processor-specific parameters. Validate against `GET /v1/processors/{name}` for the chosen version.

priority.description: Queue priority. Higher values run sooner. Subject to per-plan ceilings.

idempotency_key.description: Optional inline alternative to the `Idempotency-Key` header. If both are supplied they must be equal; otherwise the request is rejected with `400`.

Job.description: A submitted or in-flight processing job.

artifact_id.description: ID of the artifact being processed.

result.description: Processor-specific output. Populated only when `status = succeeded`.

error.description: Structured error. Populated only when `status = failed`.

attempts.description: Number of execution attempts so far (initial run counts as 1).

max_retries.description: Maximum number of additional retries after the first attempt.

cost_usd.description: Cost in US dollars accrued by this job so far.

started_at.description: When the worker first picked up the job. `null` if still queued.

completed_at.description: When the job reached a terminal state. `null` until it does.

poll_url.description: Convenience URL to `GET /v1/jobs/{id}`. Returned on creation responses; not stored long-term.

cache.description: Whether the result was served from cache.

jobs.description: Jobs to submit. Per-item validation failures are surfaced in the `rejected` array of the response; well-formed items are queued regardless.

batch_id.description: Server-assigned ID for this batch. Echoed in the `batch_id` webhook field for any `job.batch.processed` event.

accepted.description: IDs of jobs that were queued, in the same order as the corresponding `jobs` entries.

rejected.description: Per-item validation errors. `index` references the position in the request `jobs` array.

BulkRejection.description: A single per-item rejection from a bulk job submission.

index.description: Zero-based index into the request `jobs` array.

reason.description: Human-readable explanation of why the item was rejected.

url.description: HTTPS URL that will receive event deliveries. HTTP is rejected.

subscribed_events.description: Event types this endpoint wants to receive. Use `["*"]` to subscribe to every event.

description.description: Free-form label for the org's own bookkeeping.

secret.description: Optional HMAC signing secret. If omitted, Orpheus generates one and returns it on the response.

UpdateWebhookRequest.description: Request body for `PATCH /v1/webhooks/{id}`. Only present fields are modified.

active.description: When `false`, deliveries are paused without deleting the endpoint.
Whether new deliveries are currently enabled for this endpoint.

secret.description: The HMAC signing secret. Returned only on the response to `POST /v1/webhooks` when Orpheus generated it; never returned on `GET` or `PATCH`.

WebhookDelivery.description: A record of a single attempt (or replay) at delivering an event to a webhook endpoint.

event_id.description: Underlying event ID. Multiple deliveries (e.g. retries) share the same `event_id`.

attempt_count.description: Number of HTTP attempts made so far for this delivery.

last_status_code.description: HTTP status code returned by the most recent attempt.

last_error.description: Last transport-level error (DNS, TLS, timeout, etc.).

payload_url.description: URL where the event payload can be retrieved for inspection. Signed with the endpoint's secret.

WebhookEndpointList.description: List of webhook endpoints for the org.

APIKeyScope.description: A permission granted to an API key. Use `*` for full org access.

scopes.description: Permissions granted to the key. Use `["*"]` for full access.

expires_at.description: Optional expiry. If omitted, the key is valid until revoked.

APIKey.description: An API key. The `secret` field is populated **only** on the response to `POST /v1/api-keys` and is never returned by any other endpoint.

prefix.description: Public, non-secret prefix used to identify the key in logs and the dashboard.

secret.description: Full API key, including the secret portion. Returned exactly once at creation; capture it now.

last_used_at.description: When the key was last used to authenticate a request.

APIKeyList.description: List of API keys for the org. Secrets are never included.

Processor.description: A processor and its published versions.

versions.description: All published versions (including deprecated ones). Newest first.

ProcessorVersion.description: A single published version of a processor.

sunset_at.description: When this version will stop accepting new jobs. `null` for active versions.

input_schema.description: JSON Schema describing the input artifact's required properties.

params_schema.description: JSON Schema describing the accepted `params` object.

output_schema.description: JSON Schema describing the shape of `Job.result` on success.

default_params.description: Default parameter values that are merged with the user's `params` before validation.

gpu_memory_mb.description: Approximate peak GPU memory required to run this version.

cost_per_second_usd.description: Per-second GPU cost in US dollars. Billed by wall-clock time the worker holds the job.

max_audio_duration_seconds.description: Reject jobs whose input audio is longer than this. `null` means no limit.

ProcessorVersionStatus.description: Lifecycle state of a processor version.

ProcessorSummary.description: Lightweight summary of a processor returned by `GET /v1/processors`.

latest_version.description: Highest published version, regardless of status.

ProcessorList.description: List of processor summaries for the org.

Usage.description: Aggregate usage and dollar cost for a single billing period.

jobs_count.description: Number of jobs that completed (in any terminal state) during the period.

gpu_seconds.description: Total wall-clock seconds workers spent executing jobs.

storage_gb_days.description: Artifact storage consumed, in GB · days.

egress_gb.description: Bytes served via presigned download URLs, in GB.

total_usd.description: Total cost for the period in US dollars.

UsagePeriod.description: The billing period a `Usage` summary covers.

UsageBreakdown.description: A single line item in a `Usage` summary.

quantity.description: Raw quantity consumed in this category.

amount_usd.description: Cost in US dollars for this line item.

AuditLog.description: A single audit log entry recording an authenticated action.

actor_id.description: User ID for human actors, API key ID for machine actors. `null` for unauthenticated system events.

actor_type.description: Whether the actor is a human user, an API key, or a system process.

resource_type.description: Resource category affected by the action.

resource_id.description: ID of the affected resource, when applicable.

ip.description: Source IP of the request, when available.

metadata.description: Action-specific structured detail (e.g. the job's processor name and version, the API key scopes).

AuditLogList.description: Cursor-paginated list of audit log entries.
